title: Deliciously Dense Chinese Pork Scallion Pancake
description: A nutty and bouncy midnight craving Chinese dish featuring ground allspice and scallions, served on a cedar plank. High in protein and perfect for a snack.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, all-purpose flour, ground allspice, and salt, and mix until just combined.
Add the chopped scallions, rice flour, and khao khua to the bowl, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Add the eggs to the bowl, and mix until the dough comes together in a ball.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces, and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, and fry the pancakes until they are golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Serve the pancakes hot on a cedar plank, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
